title: Label units and currency in tables
when: Presenting data tables or charts with numerical values to a client or stakeholder
do: Always include units (e.g., kg, tons, hours) and currency (e.g., USD, EUR) in table headers or footnotes
dont: Show unlabeled figures or monetary amounts without currency
why: Ambiguity in units and currency leads to misinterpretation and decision errors

title: Use exact user-specified file paths
when: Reading inputs or writing outputs for a task with prescribed directories
do: Read only from input/ and write only to output/ using the exact paths specified by the user
dont: Create or write files outside the specified input/ and output/ paths
why: Automations and validators rely on predictable file locations

title: Preserve existing content when appending
when: Updating a file that already contains prior rules or entries
do: Append new entries to the end of the file without modifying or deleting any existing content
dont: Overwrite or alter previously recorded entries
why: The lessons file is append-only to protect institutional memory

title: Verify assumptions with sources before recommending
do: Validate assumptions with at least two credible sources or explicit constraints before finalizing recommendations
dont: Base recommendations on unverified assumptions or implied context
why: Verification reduces the risk of misleading or incorrect guidance

title: Write client-facing summaries in plain language
when: Delivering summaries to non-technical or mixed audiences
do: Use clear, concise, jargon-free language and define any necessary terms
dont: Use internal jargon, unexplained acronyms, or overly technical wording
why: Plain language improves clarity and trust with the audience

title: Cite sources for external data points
when: Including external statistics, market figures, or benchmarks
do: Add in-line citations or footnotes with source name and retrieval date
why: Attribution increases credibility and allows verification

title: Perform pre-decision rule scan for relevant categories
when: Before starting a task involving data presentation, communications, recommendations, or file operations
do: Scan lessons.md and top-100.md for applicable [DATA], [COMMS], [JUDGMENT], [EXEC], and [CONTEXT] rules and apply them
dont: Proceed without reviewing past lessons and community rules
why: Proactive scanning prevents repeat mistakes and improves quality

title: Prefer metric units internally
when: Working with internal calculations and datasets
do: Use SI units consistently to minimize conversion errors
dont: Mix imperial and metric units without documentation

title: Ask user for permission before community sharing
when: Preparing anonymized lessons for community submission
do: Confirm user consent before submitting any lessons externally
dont: Share lessons externally without explicit approval
why: Consent prevents unintended disclosure of preferences or processes
